_source: ServSafe certification, Restaurant management guides, Food safety regulations FDA, Kitchen operations best practices
_note: Long-tail food service knowledge. Food safety HACCP, kitchen operations, menu costing, staff management, health codes, ServSafe principles.

temperature_control.danger_zone: 41-135°F; bacteria multiply rapidly; keep food out of danger zone (cold <41°F, hot >135°F); 2-hour rule (1 hour if ambient >90°F)

cooking_temps.165f: Poultry, stuffed foods, casseroles, reheated leftovers; highest risk; 15 seconds
cooking_temps.155f: Ground meats (beef, pork, fish), eggs for hot holding, injected meats; 15 seconds
cooking_temps.145f: Whole cuts (steaks, chops, roasts), fish, eggs for immediate service; 15 seconds; allow 3-min rest time for roasts
cooking_temps.135f: Vegetables, fruits, grains, legumes cooked for hot holding; commercial food safety, not USDA recommendations

temperature_control.cooling: 135°F to 70°F in 2 hours, 70°F to 41°F in additional 4 hours (6 hours total); use ice bath, shallow pans (<2 inches deep), ice wand; monitor with thermometer; if miss window, discard
temperature_control.reheating: 165°F for 15 seconds within 2 hours; applies to all leftovers; cannot reheat more than once; use stove, oven, microwave (not steam table or slow cooker for initial reheat)
temperature_control.holding: Hot holding >135°F (steam table, warmer); cold holding <41°F (ice bath, refrigeration); check temps every 2-4 hours; no holding in danger zone

cross_contamination.color_coded_cutting_boards: Red = raw meat; Yellow = raw poultry; Green = fruits/vegetables; Blue = seafood; White = dairy/bread; prevents cross-contact
cross_contamination.separate_equipment: Dedicated cutting boards, knives, containers for raw animal products; wash between uses (raw to ready-to-eat = cross-contamination risk)
cross_contamination.hand_washing: 20 seconds with soap and water (not sanitizer); after: touching raw animal products, bathroom, eating, smoking, touching face/hair, handling trash; before: food prep, gloves
cross_contamination.glove_use: Not required but recommended; change between tasks (raw to cooked); wash hands before putting on; single-use only; not substitute for hand washing
cross_contamination.storage_order: Top to bottom by cooking temp: ready-to-eat top, seafood, whole cuts, ground meat, poultry bottom; prevents drips contaminating lower-temp foods

allergen_management.big_8_allergens: Milk, Eggs, Fish, Shellfish, Tree nuts, Peanuts, Wheat, Soybeans; plus sesame (as of 2023); 90% of allergies
allergen_management.prevention: Dedicated prep area, equipment, utensils for allergen-free; clean surfaces between; train staff to ask about allergies; clear menu labeling
allergen_management.cross_contact: Different from cross-contamination (safety vs allergen); even trace amounts trigger reactions; cannot cook out allergens; dedicated fryer for allergen-free items
allergen_management.communication: Server asks about allergies; communicates to kitchen; manager confirms with guest; separate ticket marking; verify ingredients (butter, flour coating, garnishes)

haccp.principles: Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points; systematic food safety; identify hazards, determine CCPs, establish limits, monitor, corrective actions, verify, record
haccp.critical_control_points: Cooking (temperature), cooling (time/temp), cold holding (41°F), hot holding (135°F), reheating (165°F); monitor and document
haccp.corrective_actions: If CCP not met (example: chicken cooked to 155°F not 165°F), continue cooking; if cannot correct, discard; document incident and action taken
haccp.verification: Thermometer calibration (ice point 32°F, boiling point 212°F monthly); employee training; health inspection readiness; record review

personal_hygiene.illness_policy: Exclude from work if: vomiting, diarrhea, jaundice, sore throat with fever; report to manager; return after 24 hours symptom-free (some illnesses 48 hours or doctor clearance)
personal_hygiene.hand_washing_sinks: Separate from food prep and dishwashing; hot water (100°F+), soap, paper towels; hands-free preferred (foot pedal, automatic); stocked at all times
personal_hygiene.hair_restraints: Hat, visor, hairnet, beard net; prevent hair from contacting food or food-contact surfaces; required by health codes
personal_hygiene.jewelry: Plain band ring only; no bracelets, watches, rings with stones (harbor bacteria, fall into food); remove before food prep
personal_hygiene.eating_drinking_smoking: Only in designated areas away from food prep and storage; no gum chewing in food areas; drink in covered container with straw in designated area only

mise_en_place.definition: Everything in place; prep work done before service; chop vegetables, portion proteins, prepare sauces, set up station
mise_en_place.station_setup: Line cook station; cutting board, knives, pans, spatulas, hotel pans with prepped ingredients, side towels, sanitizer bucket; efficient workflow
mise_en_place.prep_lists: Daily prep list; what needs to be prepared, quantities, priority; based on forecasted covers and 86'd items from previous day
mise_en_place.batch_cooking: Prep in batches throughout day; fresh product; avoid waste; estimate usage based on historical sales and reservations

stations.grill_broiler: Steaks, chops, chicken, fish; high heat; sear marks; doneness by touch (rare/medium-rare/medium/medium-well/well)
stations.sauté_station: Sautéed dishes, pasta, sauces; stovetop; high volume; quick cook times; coordination with other stations
stations.fry_station: Deep fryer; 350-375°F typical; french fries, fried chicken, appetizers; manage oil quality (filter daily, change when dark/smoking/foaming)
stations.pantry_cold: Salads, cold appetizers, desserts; refrigerated station; plating; garnishes; no cooking
stations.expediter_pass: Final assembly, quality check, communicate with servers, coordinate timing (all plates for table ready together), run the kitchen during service

line_cooking.ticket_management: Order comes in, call out to line, prioritize (appetizers → entrees → desserts), coordinate cook times (long-cook items fired first), all-day count (how many of each item total)
line_cooking.timing: Cook times vary; steak 6-12 min, chicken 8-12 min, fish 5-8 min, pasta 8-12 min; start longest items first; everything finishes together; communicate with team ('6 minutes out')
line_cooking.plating: Consistent presentation; protein at 6 o'clock, starch 2 o'clock, vegetable 10 o'clock; wipe plate rim; garnish; hot food hot, cold food cold; inspect before sending

inventory_management.ordering: Par levels for each item; on-hand count + usage = order quantity; daily orders (produce, dairy, bread), weekly (dry goods, frozen), monthly (paper goods); build in lead time
inventory_management.receiving: Check delivery against invoice; inspect quality (temperature, freshness, damage, expiration dates); reject if not acceptable; date and rotate stock (FIFO); store immediately
inventory_management.fifo: First In First Out; older product in front, newer in back; use oldest first; prevents spoilage; date all items when opened

storage.dry_storage: 50-70°F; dark, dry, ventilated; off floor (6 inches minimum); away from walls (to allow air circulation); rotate stock; pest control
storage.refrigeration: Walk-in 36-40°F; reach-ins 38-42°F; monitor with thermometer; store raw below cooked (as described earlier); tight wrapping; label and date
storage.freezer: 0°F or below; proper wrapping (prevent freezer burn); thaw in refrigerator (not counter); use within 3-6 months for quality

inventory_management.waste_tracking: Log wasted food (spoilage, overproduction, customer returns); cost waste; identify patterns; adjust ordering and prep; goal <4% of food cost

equipment.maintenance: Preventive maintenance schedule; clean daily (grills, fryers, ovens); weekly deep clean (walk-ins, floors, walls); monthly (hood filters, equipment service); service contracts for major equipment

common_equipment.ranges_ovens: Gas or electric; 6-burner range typical; convection oven (fan circulates, cooks faster/more evenly); standard oven; calibrate temperature monthly
common_equipment.fryers: Gas or electric; 40-50 lb capacity typical; filter oil daily (cone filters or machine); change oil when color dark, smoking point low, or foaming; temperature recovery time important
common_equipment.griddle: Flat top; breakfast (eggs, pancakes), burgers; season before use (oil coating); clean with grill brick; maintain temperature zones (high, medium, low)
common_equipment.charbroiler: Open flame grill; radiant heat; grill marks; clean grates daily; ceramic briquettes or lava rocks (replace when deteriorated)
common_equipment.reach_in_coolers_freezers: Undercounter or upright; quick access; monitor temps; gaskets seal tight (replace if torn); defrost freezers regularly
common_equipment.dishwasher: High-temp (180°F sanitize, requires booster heater) or low-temp (chemical sanitize); 1-3 minute cycle; scrape/rinse before loading; clean throughout day

equipment.safety: Grease fires (Class K extinguisher, never water); equipment guards in place; training on operation; lockout-tagout for repairs; report damage immediately

menu_development.balance: Variety of proteins, preparations, flavors, textures; dietary options (vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free); seasonality (fresh, local ingredients when possible)
menu_development.item_profitability: Stars (high profit, high popularity), Plowhorses (low profit, high popularity - keep for volume), Puzzles (high profit, low popularity - promote or replace), Dogs (low profit, low popularity - remove)
menu_development.menu_psychology: Placement (top right corner = prime real estate), boxes/borders (draw attention), descriptions (sensory words increase sales 27%), avoid $ symbols (increases price sensitivity)
menu_development.pricing_strategy: Anchor pricing (expensive item makes others seem reasonable), bundle pricing (prix fixe, combo meals), odd pricing ($12.95 vs $13), competitive analysis

recipe_costing.ingredient_costing: Purchase price / yield = cost per usable unit; example: $30 for 10 lb chicken, yields 6.5 lb after butchering = $4.62/lb usable; cost each ingredient in recipe
recipe_costing.portion_costing: Sum all ingredient costs for recipe; divide by number of portions = cost per portion; example: pasta recipe costs $18, makes 8 servings = $2.25 per serving
recipe_costing.ideal_food_cost: 30-35% typical full-service restaurant; 25-30% fine dining (higher menu prices); 20-25% fast food; Portion cost / Menu price = food cost %; example: $2.25 portion cost / $12.95 menu price = 17.4%
recipe_costing.cost_markup: Portion cost × markup = menu price; typical markup 3-4× in full-service (1 / target food cost %); example: $2.25 × 3.5 = $7.88, round to $8.95 menu price

yield_testing.ap_vs_ep: AP = As Purchased weight; EP = Edible Portion weight after trim/cooking; Yield % = EP / AP × 100
yield_testing.examples: Lettuce 75% yield (25% waste from core, outer leaves); Beef tenderloin 60% yield (trimming fat, silver skin); Chicken 65% yield (bones, skin, fat)
yield_testing.cooking_loss: Meat shrinks during cooking; 25-30% weight loss typical (moisture evaporation, fat rendering); factor into portion sizing; 6 oz raw = 4.5 oz cooked

prime_cost.definition: COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) + Labor = Prime Cost; largest expenses; target 60-65% of revenue
prime_cost.cogs: (Beginning inventory + Purchases - Ending inventory) / Revenue × 100 = Food Cost %; track weekly; goal 28-35%
prime_cost.labor_cost: (Gross payroll + benefits) / Revenue × 100 = Labor Cost %; goal 25-35%; varies by service level (fine dining higher, fast food lower)
prime_cost.controlling: Reduce waste, portion control, prevent theft (food and time), efficient scheduling, menu engineering (promote high-profit items)

revenue_forecasting.covers: Number of guests; track daily, weekly, monthly; identify patterns (days, seasons, events, weather); historical average + trends + events = forecast
revenue_forecasting.per_person_average: Total revenue / covers = PPA or check average; increase through upselling (appetizers, desserts, beverages, premium items)
revenue_forecasting.revenue_centers: Food revenue, beverage revenue (alcohol, non-alcohol), catering, retail; track separately; different margins

break_even_analysis.fixed_costs: Rent, insurance, salaries (management), loan payments, utilities (base), licenses; remain constant regardless of sales
break_even_analysis.variable_costs: COGS, hourly labor, supplies (to-go containers, napkins), credit card fees; increase with sales
break_even_analysis.break_even_point: Fixed costs / (1 - Variable cost %) = Break-even revenue; example: $30k fixed, 55% variable cost = $30k / 0.45 = $66,667 monthly revenue to break even

profit_and_loss.cogs: Food cost + beverage cost; subtract from revenue = Gross profit
profit_and_loss.labor: Wages, payroll taxes, benefits; subtract from gross profit
profit_and_loss.operating_expenses: Rent, utilities, marketing, supplies, repairs, insurance; subtract
profit_and_loss.ebitda: Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, Amortization; operating profit; goal 10-15% of revenue in full-service
profit_and_loss.net_profit: After all expenses; goal 5-10% of revenue (varies widely); independent restaurants 3-5% average, franchises/chains 6-9%

hiring.positions: Front of house (servers, hosts, bartenders, bussers); Back of house (cooks, prep cooks, dishwashers); Management (general manager, kitchen manager, FOH manager)
hiring.interview: Ask about availability, experience, reason for leaving last job, scenarios (difficult customer, mistake on order); trail shift (working interview, 2-4 hours, observe skills)
hiring.onboarding: I-9 employment verification, W-4, direct deposit, handbook review, sexual harassment training, food safety training, menu knowledge, POS training, shadow experienced staff

scheduling.labor_budget: Target labor % (25-35% of revenue); forecast revenue, multiply by target % = labor budget dollars; divide by average hourly wage = labor hours available
scheduling.coverage: Peak hours need more staff; lunch 11-2, dinner 5-9; slower times skeleton crew; day of week matters (Friday/Saturday busiest, Monday/Tuesday slowest); adjust based on forecast
scheduling.positions_needed: 1 server per 3-4 tables (full service), 1 cook per station, 1 expediter, 1 busser per 2-3 servers, 1 dishwasher minimum (2 if high volume), host, bartender; scale to covers
scheduling.shift_types: AM (prep and lunch), PM (prep and dinner), double (all day), split (AM and PM with break), on-call (called in if busy); 2-week schedule posted in advance

training.foh_training: Menu knowledge (ingredients, allergies, descriptions), POS system, upselling, service steps (greet, drinks, apps, entrees, check), handling complaints, alcohol service (check ID, recognize intoxication, cut off)
training.boh_training: Food safety, recipes, cooking techniques, equipment operation, knife skills, station procedures, ticket reading, timing, communication
training.ongoing: Menu changes, new recipes, quarterly food safety review, performance feedback, cross-training (build versatility)

motivation_retention.turnover: Industry average 70-80% annually; expensive (recruiting, training costs); reduce with competitive pay, positive culture, growth opportunities, recognition
motivation_retention.incentives: Employee meal discounts (50% or free shift meal), tips (servers, bartenders), bonuses (kitchen, managers - monthly or quarterly), contests (most desserts sold, perfect attendance)
motivation_retention.culture: Respect all positions, teamwork, open communication, family meal (pre-shift meal together), celebrate successes, provide feedback (positive and constructive)

table_service_steps.greeting: Within 1 minute of seating; introduce self, specials, drink order; make eye contact, smile, welcoming
table_service_steps.drinks: Deliver within 3-5 minutes; take appetizer or entree order if ready; suggest cocktails, wine, specialty drinks (upsell)
table_service_steps.order_taking: Suggest appetizers, sides, wine pairings; repeat order for accuracy; note allergies/modifications; submit to kitchen promptly; enter correctly in POS
table_service_steps.course_timing: Appetizers within 10 min of order; entrees 15-20 min; check back within 2 min of delivery ('How is everything?'); refill drinks proactively
table_service_steps.check_presentation: Present when requested or after clearing entree plates; return promptly with change or card; thank guest; invite back

upselling.techniques: Suggestive selling (not pushy); appetizers ('Our calamari is fantastic'), premium proteins ('Upgrade to filet mignon for $8'), desserts ('Save room for our famous cheesecake'), cocktails ('Our mojitos are refreshing')
upselling.impact: Increases check average 15-30%; small incremental sales add up; benefits server (tips), kitchen (higher revenue), owner (profit)

handling_complaints.listen: Let guest speak without interruption; empathize ('I understand, I would be upset too'); apologize (even if not restaurant's fault)
handling_complaints.resolve: Offer solution (remake dish, discount, comped item, manager visit); empower staff to comp up to $25 without manager approval (speeds resolution)
handling_complaints.follow_up: Check back after resolution ('Is everything better now?'); ensure satisfaction; note in POS for manager review

responsible_service.legal_drinking_age: 21 in US; check ID for anyone appearing under 30-40 (house policy); acceptable IDs: driver's license, state ID, passport, military ID; verify photo, DOB, not expired
responsible_service.signs_of_intoxication: Slurred speech, glassy eyes, impaired coordination, loud/obnoxious behavior, drowsiness; if observed, slow service (water, food), cut off if necessary, offer taxi/Uber
responsible_service.cutting_off: Private conversation; 'I cannot serve you more alcohol; I can call you a ride'; do not argue; involve manager; guest safety priority; liability for overserving (dram shop laws)
responsible_service.serving_limits: 1 drink per person at a time (no doubles), no alcohol to minors or intoxicated persons (law), last call 30-60 min before close (state law varies)

bartending_basics.standard_pour: 1.5 oz liquor (jigger or count pour 4-5 seconds); 5 oz wine; 12 oz beer; standardize for consistency and cost control
bartending_basics.inventory_control: Par stock, bottle counts, daily reconciliation (sales vs depletion); identify theft or over-pouring; liquor cost 18-24% goal
bartending_basics.cocktails: Know classic cocktails (Martini, Manhattan, Margarita, Old Fashioned, Mojito); use recipes for consistency; fresh ingredients for quality

inspection_preparation.daily_readiness: Clean as you go; food properly labeled/dated/stored; temps logged; hand sinks stocked; no bare-hand contact with RTE foods; employees healthy
inspection_preparation.inspection_focus: Food temperatures, cross-contamination prevention, hand washing, food storage, cleanliness, pest control, equipment function, employee knowledge

common_violations.critical: Improper holding temps (most common), inadequate cooking temps, bare-hand contact with ready-to-eat, ill employee working; can cause foodborne illness; must correct immediately
common_violations.non_critical: Inadequate cleaning, missing labels/dates, minor equipment damage; doesn't cause immediate illness risk; correct within timeframe (days to weeks)

inspection_outcome.pass: No critical violations or corrected on-site; routine follow-up
inspection_outcome.conditional_pass: Critical violations noted; must correct within 3-10 days; re-inspection
inspection_outcome.fail: Imminent health hazard (no hot water, sewage backup, pest infestation); shut down until corrected; re-inspection required before reopening

employment_law.minimum_wage: Federal $7.25 (as of 2024); many states higher; tipped employees $2.13 federal (employer makes up difference if tips don't reach minimum wage)
employment_law.overtime: 1.5× regular rate for hours >40 per week; some states daily overtime; salaried exempt employees (managers >$684/week + duties test) no overtime
employment_law.breaks: Federal law doesn't require; many states mandate (10-min paid break per 4 hours, 30-min unpaid meal break per 6 hours); check state law

licenses_permits.business_license: City/county; required to operate; annual renewal; $50-500
licenses_permits.food_service_permit: Health department; inspection required; annual fee; $200-1000
licenses_permits.liquor_license: State ABC (Alcohol Beverage Control); expensive ($5,000-$500,000 depending on state, type, transferability); beer/wine vs full liquor; on-premise vs off-premise; strict regulations
licenses_permits.food_handler_cards: Employees certified in food safety; $15-30; online course and test; renew every 3-5 years; manager food safety certification (ServSafe Manager) required in many jurisdictions

liability_insurance.liquor_liability: Dram shop coverage; overserving claims; $1-2M; $1,000-10,000/year (varies widely by state, sales volume)
liability_insurance.workers_compensation: Required in all states except TX; covers employee injuries on job; rates vary by state and risk classification (kitchen higher than office)
